evals.prompt: Load the Cora citation dataset and train a two-layer GCN for node classification, using the train mask for the loss and reporting test accuracy.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-torch-geometric: loads Planetoid(root=..., name='Cora'), defines a GCN with two GCNConv layers and log_softmax, trains with F.nll_loss on out[data.train_mask], and reports accuracy on data.test_mask. Uses PyG's Data object (data.x, data.edge_index).

assertions.value: Uses torch_geometric Planetoid/Cora and GCNConv layers with train_mask/test_mask masking rather than a generic dense network.

evals.prompt: I want to classify whole molecules from the ENZYMES dataset. Build a GNN that pools node features to a graph-level embedding and train it with mini-batching.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-torch-geometric: loads TUDataset(name='ENZYMES'), batches with torch_geometric.loader.DataLoader, builds a model with GCNConv layers and global_mean_pool over data.batch to get graph-level embeddings, and trains for graph classification with nll_loss on batch.y.

assertions.value: Uses a graph-level readout (global_mean_pool over data.batch) and the PyG DataLoader for mini-batching multiple graphs, loading TUDataset/ENZYMES.

evals.prompt: Set up a Graph Attention Network with multi-head attention for node classification on CiteSeer, with dropout on the attention.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-torch-geometric: loads Planetoid CiteSeer, defines a model with GATConv layers (multiple heads, dropout), applying elu activations and log_softmax, and trains node classification. Uses attention-based PyG convolution.

assertions.value: Uses GATConv with multiple attention heads and dropout (PyG attention layer) rather than a plain GCN or non-PyG attention implementation.

evals.prompt: I have a knowledge graph with paper and author nodes and 'writes' and 'cites' edges. Set up a heterogeneous GNN over these multiple node and edge types.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-torch-geometric: represents the graph with HeteroData (data['paper'].x, data['author'].x, typed edge_index like ('author','writes','paper')), and builds a heterogeneous GNN via to_hetero on a homogeneous model or HeteroConv with per-edge-type convolutions.

assertions.value: Uses HeteroData with typed nodes/edges and a heterogeneous model (to_hetero or HeteroConv) rather than collapsing the graph to a single homogeneous type.

evals.prompt: I just need to compute betweenness centrality and detect communities on my collaboration graph and visualize it - no machine learning, just classical graph metrics.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-torch-geometric. Computing classical centrality and community-detection metrics and drawing the graph is plain graph analytics with no learned model, which is alterlab-networkx territory rather than GNN/geometric deep learning. Should defer to alterlab-networkx.
